en: April, that is not a very good attitude. I will keep my eye on you.
From my circular desk where I can see everything, tiny swivel.
ko: 내가 모든 것을 볼 수 있는 원형 책상에서, 작은 회전 의자.

en: See how it works? What about my office and its many walls?
That becomes a new public waiting room.
And we got rid of that giant pillar that was in front of your door.
ko: 그리고 너의 문 앞에 있던 거대한 기둥을 없앴어.

en: I loved that pillar. It made it really annoying to stand in my doorway.
Well, get over it, because it's gone. You're going to be more accessible than ever.
Excuse me. There's a sign at Ramsit Park that says, do not drink the sprinkler water.
ko: 램싯 공원에 물뿌리개 물을 마시지 말라는 표지가 있어.

en: So I made sun tea with it and now I have an infection.
ko: 그래서 그걸로 선차를 만들었고, 지금 감염이 생겼어.

en: Sir? Sir? Are you listening to me, sir?
Sir, I'm talking to you! Sir?
ko: 선생님, 당신의 수돗물에 오염물이 있다는 걸 알고 계신가요?

en: Sir, are you aware that there is waste in your water system?
ko: 이건 잘 안 될 것 같아.

en: Okay, you did not just swivel away while I was talking to you.
ko: 알겠어, 내가 너와 이야기하는 동안 너가 그냥 돌아서지 않았어.
